verdict_reasoning: EREG encodes epiregulin, an EGF-family growth factor synthesized as a type-I single-pass transmembrane precursor (pro-epiregulin) anchored at the plasma membrane before regulated ectodomain shedding by ADAM proteases releases the soluble growth factor. The membrane-anchored precursor supports juxtacrine signaling from the cell surface, but this TM stage is transient — the dominant biological form is the shed soluble ligand that acts on EGFR/ERBB4. This matches the 'transient TM precursor of a shed-ligand-dominant gene' pattern: real surface stage qualifies as contextual/dual_localization rather than yes or secreted_only.
